frameworks.name: disinhibition circuit motif
frameworks.one_line: Inhibition of inhibitory neurons as a fast switch for network state and function.

frameworks.name: attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Flexible attractors generated by disinhibition support persistent working-memory-like activity.

frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: Value normalization reproduced without a dedicated normalization circuit.

frameworks.name: winner-take-all competition
frameworks.one_line: WTA choice dynamics emerge from the same disinhibition mechanism.

core_question: Can a single disinhibition-based circuit motif unify value normalization, winner-take-all choice, and flexible persistent activity in decision-making?
key_assumptions: inhibitory neurons can shape circuit function via disinhibition, not just suppression
local circuit motifs can be reconfigured by top-down input to switch dynamical regime
neurophysiological value coding and psychometric choice share a common circuit substrate
manipulations: top-down drive to disinhibitory interneurons (regime switch)
input value structure to fit empirical neurophysiology and choice data
task context to elicit different forms of persistent activity
key_findings: disinhibition model reproduces both value normalization and winner-take-all dynamics in one circuit
model exhibits flexible attractor dynamics consistent with diverse persistent-activity patterns
fits empirical value-coding neurophysiology and psychometric choice behavior
top-down disinhibition supports task-dependent reconfiguration of neural dynamics
interpretations: local disinhibition is a biologically plausible unifying mechanism for decision-making
previously separate decision-circuit models can be reconciled under a single disinhibitory motif
